summary_one_line: Input must drive the Content-Disposition value to exactly equal the string "attachment" so curl_strequal's case-insensitive compare succeeds.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A parsed mime/header configuration whose disposition string equals the exact literal "attachment" (10 bytes, case-insensitive), so that curl_strequal(disposition, "attachment") returns true and the true branch at mime.c:1830 is taken; the winning corpus reaches it via a structured Content-Type header section that lets disposition be set and compared against the embedded literal.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry the structured header bytes 'Content-Typ' at offsets 0x24+ and a populated header region (e.g. 'Content-Type:where'); the blocker true side is reached 28 times by W and 0 by L, and W spends far less time blocked (cmplog 6.20h vs naive 22.50h). I2S in cmplog/vpc copies the 'attachment' operand recorded by the strcmp/strequal CMP callback directly into the disposition bytes, manufacturing the exact match havoc almost never stumbles onto.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds (naive/value_profile) are short 42/38/44-byte inputs whose header region is absent or garbage (e.g. '------d', '%A&%%c%dC', 'reee/rrr'); they never present a disposition equal to "attachment", so curl_strequal returns false (L hits the true side 0 times, false side 21) and the branch stays blocked for ~21-22h.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace: the curl_strequal/strcmp callback records the literal operand "attachment", and I2SRandReplace substitutes it into the input at the disposition offset, satisfying the exact-string equality. naive and plain value_profile lack I2SRandReplace, so they must blindly mutate the multi-byte literal and fail.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch requires disposition to be equal to the literal string "attachment" via curl_strequal.
Both decisive pairs differ by exactly the I2S technique, attributing the divergence to I2S.
The second decisive pair value_profile_cmplog > value_profile is also an I2S delta.
Winner-resolving seeds contain a structured Content-Type header region the loser seeds lack.
Loser-blocking seeds are short garbage inputs without a coherent header section.
Winner reaches the blocker true side many times while the loser never does.
Winner is blocked far less time than loser, consistent with I2S quickly manufacturing the match.
cmplog and value_profile_cmplog add I2SRandReplace which copies a recorded CMP operand into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 277 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 3ada62fdafca01cd`; observed two I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 18 and 19 of the 20-level ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog/vpc.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the true branch on curl_strequal(x, "attachment") in which cmplog/vpc resolve at the same rate as naive/value_profile (no I2S advantage), or in which the loser fuzzers resolve it within the campaign budget.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset where the disposition string is set and compared is inferred from the Content-Type header path, not directly pinpointed by a single concentrated W-vs-L byte offset carrying the literal "attachment".
